title: Rule Evaluation Result
description: v1 canonical contract for one deterministic rule-evaluation result over a single property (the exported shape of app.rules.integration.PropertyRuleEvaluation; the fulfilment of the README-deferred 'rule evaluation trace (M4)' contract). Draft-rule output is a candidate representation, NEVER a Verified legal determination: coverage_status is drawn from the canonical PRD-section-12 vocabulary MINUS 'verified' (a draft result may never be Verified before published-rule + G6 qualified-human approval, PRD sections 10-12), and every result carries not_verified_disclaimer and professional_review_required. The evaluated INPUT is identified by reference - bbl, the evaluated property_profile contract_version, the input provenance_refs (which resolve into that profile's provenance array), and a deterministic input_fingerprint - so this document NEVER embeds a full copy of the property profile (owner directive, M4-T005). Spatial uncertainty (M2-T013 share ranges, positional classes, review flags) is preserved as structured data, never collapsed into a definitive single-district assignment. The canonical coverage vocabulary is REFERENCED from coverage_status.schema.json and NEVER redefined inline; shared value shapes (bbl, non_empty_string, digest_sha256, data_completeness) are $ref'd from common.schema.json / coverage_status.schema.json. A later aggregate-analysis contract can embed or reference this self-contained, versioned document without breaking it.

contract_version.description: CLOSED enum of published rule_evaluation contract versions. 1.0.0 = M4-T005 initial shape. New versions are admitted only by an accepted contract task that appends to this enum; arbitrary versions are rejected. This is the version of THIS contract, distinct from evaluated_input.profile_contract_version (the version of the property_profile that was evaluated).

coverage_source.description: PLATFORM-DEFINED: whether this coverage label came from a deterministic evaluator trace (rule_evaluator) or an integration fail-safe short-circuit (integration_fail_safe). Grounded in app.rules.integration._COVERAGE_SOURCE_EVALUATOR / _COVERAGE_SOURCE_FAIL_SAFE.

data_completeness.description: Most-severe data-completeness across the evaluated traces (exactly the 3 PRD s12 values, referenced from coverage_status.schema.json, never redefined), or null on a fail-safe short-circuit that produced no trace.

needs_review.description: True when the result requires human review: a draft (non-published) rule, professional-review coverage, a data conflict, or a professional-review spatial rollup. A draft result is never auto-final.

professional_review_required.description: True when coverage_status is professional_review_required (or a fail-safe forced it). Deterministic escalation to a qualified human, never an AI decision.

fail_safe.description: True when the result is a fail-safe short-circuit (no computed value, no guessed district) rather than a completed evaluator run.

fail_safe_reason.description: Machine-readable fail-safe discriminator (finer than coverage_status) when fail_safe is true; null otherwise. Grounded in the app.rules.integration.FAILSAFE_* constants.

rule_lifecycle_statuses.description: Sorted, de-duplicated lifecycle statuses of the rules that were evaluated (PRD sections 10-11). Empty on a fail-safe short-circuit. 'published' may appear, but coverage_status can still never be 'verified' in this contract (a Verified determination requires the separate G6 approval attached, which this draft-integration document never carries).

not_verified_disclaimer.description: The permanent honest disclaimer stamped on every result (app.rules.integration.NOT_VERIFIED_DISCLAIMER). Its human-readable text contains the word 'Verified'; that text is NEVER a coverage_status and never up-labels the result.

zoning_district.description: The single confidently-assigned base-zoning district the evaluation used, or null on any fail-safe (an uncertain lot is never collapsed into a definitive district).

lot_area_sq_ft.description: The zoning-lot area input used, strictly positive, or null when no positive area was available (the evaluator then yields a missing-critical result and no computed value).

lot_area_source.description: Which profile field supplied lot_area_sq_ft (e.g. 'lot_geometry.area_sq_ft' or 'spatial_intersection.pairs[].lot_area_sq_ft'), or null when no area was used. Open string (not a closed enum) so a future area source lands without a contract bump.

spatial_context.description: The compact spatial summary the evaluator saw, or null when no spatial substrate was present.

evaluations.description: Deterministic evaluator traces (already provenance-resolved by RuleResult.export()); empty on a fail-safe short-circuit. Each trace carries citations, computation steps, outputs, determination, and propagated uncertainty.

reasons.description: Human-readable reasons for the outcome (fail-safe cause, not_applicable explanation, etc.). Never a substitute for the machine-readable fields above.

rule_conflict.description: Typed same-family rule-conflict object (competing rule IDs + each rule's effective window) when >=2 same-family rules are simultaneously in effect and independently applicable for overlapping outputs (a legal ambiguity surfaced for professional review); null otherwise. Never selects, ranks, or merges rules, and never carries a computed value.

_expected_failure.description: FIXTURE-ONLY annotation stating the intended defect of an invalid/ fixture (packages/contracts README convention). The evaluator NEVER emits this key; it is allowed here (optional) so an invalid fixture fails only for its stated defect under the additionalProperties:false guard, and so valid fixtures - which omit it - stay clean.

coverage_status_draft.description: The canonical PRD-section-12 coverage vocabulary (REFERENCED from coverage_status.schema.json, never redefined) NARROWED to exclude 'verified'. A draft-rule result may never be Verified (owner directive, M4-T005; PRD sections 10-12). The allOf keeps coverage_status.schema.json the single source of the vocabulary while the subset enum removes exactly 'verified'.

evaluated_input.description: Identity of the evaluated property input BY REFERENCE - never an embedded copy of the property profile (owner directive, M4-T005). bbl + the evaluated profile contract_version + the input provenance_refs (resolving into that profile's provenance array) + a deterministic input_fingerprint pin exactly which input produced this result.

bbl.description: BBL of the evaluated property, or null when the profile carried no identity.bbl.

profile_contract_version.description: The contract_version of the property_profile instance that was evaluated (e.g. '1.4.0'). Intentionally an open non-empty string rather than a closed enum, so it is not coupled to the property_profile contract's own evolving published-version list; the authoritative closed enum lives in property_profile.schema.json.

input_fingerprint.description: Deterministic fingerprint (canonical sha256 digest, common.schema.json digest_sha256 shape) of the canonical evaluator inputs (zoning_district, lot_area_sq_ft, as_of_date, and the identifying spatial facts). Lets a consumer confirm a result was produced from a specific input snapshot WITHOUT storing the profile. The exact canonicalization/computation is implemented in a later phase (M4-T005 phase 2); this contract defines the field and its hex-digest shape now.

input_provenance.description: Which property_profile provenance records back each derived evaluator input. Each entry is a list of provenance_ref ids that MUST resolve to a provenance_id in the evaluated profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19) - the same provenance-ref-by-string pattern property_profile uses (a source_fact record per id). Empty lists are legal on a fail-safe with no derived input.

spatial_context.description: The compact spatial summary passed to the evaluator (a subset of spatial_uncertainty).

spatial_uncertainty.description: The M2-T013 facts-with-uncertainty preserved verbatim - share RANGES, positional classes, and review flags - NEVER collapsed into a single definitive district. Present (with empty/null members) even on a fail-safe.

crosscheck.description: The M2-T008 lot-level zoning cross-check outcome carried verbatim, or null. Open shape (the cross-check contract is owned by the spatial substrate); consumers narrow at the use site.

base_district_candidate.description: One base-zoning candidate pair with its split-share RANGE preserved. minor_portion flags a minor share of a split lot. Fields are nullable because a malformed foreign spatial record must be surfaced, never dropped.

family_coverage.description: Coverage honesty for the evaluated rule family (app.rules.registry.family_coverage): a family with no implemented rule is a VISIBLE 'unsupported'; a family with draft rules is 'conditional' pending G6. coverage_status is drawn from the same never-Verified vocabulary.

rule_ids.description: Present only for a supported family; the rule IDs that back the coverage.

rule_conflict.description: Typed same-family rule conflict (app.rules.registry.detect_conflicts). Surfaces the competing rules and their effective windows; NEVER picks a governing rule (that is a legal determination) and never produces a value from the competing rules.

evaluation_trace.description: One exported deterministic rule-evaluation trace (mirrors the rule-engine-internal services/api/app/rules/schemas/v1/evaluation_trace.schema.json, the authoritative export shape), with coverage_status narrowed to the never-Verified vocabulary for this draft-integration contract. Records resolved inputs, the applicability decision, every computation step with resolved args and result, the citations WITH their source-snapshot provenance (a material value is never exported without it, PRD section 19), the coverage + data-completeness labels, and any propagated M2-T013 geometric uncertainty.

input_validation.description: Fail-closed input validation result. When valid is false the evaluation stops before computation and coverage is professional_review_required (no computed value).

value_seen.description: The rejected value as seen (any JSON type).

rule_release.description: The rule's test/lifecycle release status carried per evaluation. verified_eligible is true only for a published rule evaluated with a matching G6 approval attached - which this draft-integration contract never carries, so coverage never reaches 'verified'.

effective_window.description: Temporal effectiveness of this rule version relative to the evaluation as_of date. evaluated_as_of is null when the caller passed none (no temporal gating).

determination.description: Compliance pass/fail determination when the rule declares one and was applicable+computed; null otherwise. Never changes coverage.

citation.description: One source citation WITH its resolved source-snapshot provenance (a material value may never be exported without it, PRD section 19).
